news.title: ASML Delivers First High-NA EUV Lithography System to Intel's Oregon R&D Center
news.summary: Intel has received its first Twinscan EXE:5000 High-NA EUV system, initiating critical calibration runs for sub-2nm digital silicon nodes. The machine utilizes a 0.55 numerical aperture projection lens, achieving 8nm line-space resolution for advanced high-volume manufacturing patterns.
news.whyItMatters: This system marks a massive architectural transition in micro-lithography, moving beyond multi-patterning techniques and enabling direct resolution of dense copper networks. This physically circumvents physical overlay errors and significantly improves wafer throughput.
news.industryImpact: Enables the commercialization of Intel's 14A node, paving the way for hyper-dense gate-all-around standard cell libraries with 15% reduction in mask steps.
news.author: Dr. Evelyn Vance

news.title: NVIDIA Blackwell B200 Superchips Complete Initial HBM3e Tapeout Verification
news.summary: NVIDIA's next-generation Blackwell B200 hardware accelerator has completed physical timing and voltage drop verification across sub-die interposer boundary routes. The chip package consolidates over 208 billion transistors on a dual-die layout connected by a ultra-low-latency 10TB/s interface.
news.whyItMatters: Eliminates the typical off-chip memory bandwidth bottleneck by stacking 192GB of HBM3e memory vertically. This provides unprecedented dot-product computation speeds directly adjacent to the tensor execution array.
news.industryImpact: Delivers a 4x reduction in total cost of ownership and power consumption for continuous LLM token generation at enterprise scales.
news.author: Marcus Thorne

news.title: OpenLane v2.5 Adds Advanced CTS and Dynamic IR-Drop Synthesizer
news.summary: The OpenLane open-source RTL-to-GDSII flow has been updated with a fully parallelized Clock Tree Synthesis (CTS) planner and interactive power grid IR-drop validation. Built on top of the OpenROAD optimizer, it automates cell sizing to close timing corners.
news.whyItMatters: Democratizes custom application-specific integrated circuit (ASIC) engineering by replacing multi-million dollar license barriers with a robust, community-validated, open-source physical design toolchain.
news.industryImpact: Reduces standard cell synthesis turn-around times by 40% for educational projects targeting SkyWater 130nm and GlobalFoundries 180nm shuttle runs.
news.author: Elena Rostova
